title: Badges and Gamification Framework
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to earn badges as I reach milestones in the application, so that I feel rewarded for exploring features and am motivated to deepen my usage of the product.
description:
Build a reusable badges and gamification framework that awards users badges based on actions they perform in the application. The system tracks user actions, evaluates badge criteria, and awards badges with a celebratory confetti animation.

### Architecture
- **badges** table: stores badge definitions (name, description, image)
- **badge_triggers** table: defines actions required to earn each badge (badge ID, action name, required count)
- **user_actions** table: logs user actions with incremental counts
- **user_badges** table: tracks which badges each user has earned
- **BadgeService**: centralized service handling action logging, criteria checking, and badge awarding

### Badge Triggers (Initial Set)
1. **Early Explorer** — user adds their 3rd Salesforce object to the dictionary
2. **ERD** — user accesses a view on the ERD tab
3. **Initiative Tracker** — user enters their 2nd initiative with required fields filled
4. **Pro-Active Collaborator** — user invites another user into DataTools Pro
5. **Team Builder** — 5 invited users have successfully been added
6. **Migration Pro** — user marks an object migration status as complete

### Algorithm
1. When a user performs an action, log it in `user_actions` (increment count if exists)
2. After logging, evaluate all badges: check if user meets all trigger criteria
3. If criteria met and badge not already awarded, insert into `user_badges`
4. Display confetti animation on new badge award

### Super Admin
- New `is_admin` field on user model (boolean, default false)
- Super admin middleware restricts badge management pages
- Super admin UI for creating badges, defining triggers, and managing badge images

### User-Facing UX
- Settings page shows earned badges (full color) and unearned badges (grayed out)
- New badge award triggers a screen pop with confetti animation
- Future: link to relevant page from unearned badges, Trailhead-style progression
acceptance_criteria: Users earn badges automatically when trigger criteria are met
Badge award triggers a confetti animation screen pop
Settings page displays earned badges in color and unearned badges grayed out
Multiple badges can be awarded simultaneously from a single action
Badges are only awarded once per user (no duplicates)
Super admin can create and manage badges and triggers via the admin UI
Action logging tracks user interactions with incremental counts
All 6 initial badge triggers are functional
ux_changes: New badges section in settings displaying earned (color) and unearned (grayed out) badges. Confetti animation pop-up when a new badge is awarded. Super admin pages for badge and trigger management.
middleware_changes: BadgeService handles action logging, criteria evaluation, and badge awarding. Super admin middleware restricts badge management routes to users with is_admin=true.
data_model_changes: Four new tables: badges (name, description, image), badge_triggers (badge_id, action_name, action_count), user_actions (user_id, action_name, count), user_badges (user_id, badge_id). New is_admin boolean field on the users table.
implementation_notes: Designed as a reusable module that could be packaged for other applications. Badge images stored via Laravel storage with symlink (php artisan storage:link). Initially seeded via super admin UI; future consideration for Trailhead-style progression linking from unearned badges to relevant pages. The 'Team Builder' badge (5 accepted invites) awards from backend without confetti since the action is passive.
comment_summary: Developer proposed a comprehensive badge architecture with 4 DB tables and a BadgeService. Product owner clarified 'admin' vs 'super admin' terminology and approved the UX concept showing earned/unearned badges. 5 of 6 badges were tested and working. Super admin management UI was built with badge creation, trigger configuration, and image upload. Future improvements discussed include Trailhead-style progression links.
